reasoning: The control IA-5(16) focuses on authenticator management, specifically requiring in-person or trusted external party authentication. This is primarily related to identity management and password policies, which are managed at the cloud provider level (e.g., IAM password policies in AWS, Azure AD password policies). Kubernetes itself does not manage user authentication in the same way as cloud IAM services. Kubernetes can integrate with external identity providers for authentication, but it does not directly manage password policies or enforce in-person authentication. Therefore, this control is not applicable to Kubernetes security checks.
title: Authenticator Management - Authenticator management | in - person or trusted external party authenticator i
